nouns.meaning: A small furry domestic animal with whiskers that says meow
nouns.example: My cat sleeps on my bed every night and purrs when I pet her soft fur.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A cat is a soft, furry pet that says 'meow' and likes to chase mice.
nouns.fun_fact: Cats sleep for about 13 to 16 hours every day!

nouns.meaning: A friendly domestic animal that barks and loves to play fetch
nouns.example: My dog wags his tail and jumps with joy when I come home from school.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A dog is a pet that barks 'woof woof' and loves to play and go for walks.
nouns.fun_fact: Dogs can understand up to 250 words and gestures!

nouns.meaning: An animal with feathers, wings, and a beak that can fly
nouns.example: The red cardinal bird sits on the tree branch and sings a beautiful, pretty song.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A bird is an animal that has feathers, wings, and can fly in the sky.
nouns.fun_fact: Hummingbirds can fly backwards and flap their wings 80 times per second!

nouns.meaning: An animal that lives in water, breathes with gills, and has fins
nouns.example: The orange goldfish swims around the little castle inside its fish tank.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A fish is an animal that lives in water, breathes underwater, and has fins to swim.
nouns.fun_fact: The largest fish is the whale shark, which can be as long as a school bus!

nouns.meaning: A building where a person or family lives
nouns.example: My house has a bright red door and a big backyard with a swing set for playing.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A house is a building where you and your family live, eat, and sleep.
nouns.fun_fact: The first houses were made from animal skins, wood, and even ice!

nouns.meaning: A set of rooms for living in a larger building
nouns.example: We live on the third floor of our apartment building, and we have an elevator.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: An apartment is a home inside a big building with many other homes.
nouns.fun_fact: Some apartment buildings are so tall they touch the clouds!

nouns.meaning: A four-wheeled vehicle that drives on roads to take people places
nouns.example: My dad drives our blue car to work every morning, and I ride in the back seat.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A car is a machine with wheels that you drive to go to school, the store, or far places.
nouns.fun_fact: The fastest car in the world can go over 300 miles per hour!

nouns.meaning: A large vehicle that carries many people on fixed routes
nouns.example: I take the yellow school bus to school every day, and I sit with my best friend.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A bus is a big vehicle that carries lots of people at the same time.
nouns.fun_fact: The longest bus in the world can carry over 250 people!

nouns.meaning: A connected line of vehicles that moves on railroad tracks
nouns.example: We took a train to visit Grandma, and I watched the trees and houses zoom by the window.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A train is a long line of cars that moves on special metal tracks.
nouns.fun_fact: The longest train ever was over 4 miles long!

nouns.meaning: A two-wheeled vehicle you ride by pushing pedals with your feet
nouns.example: I ride my red bicycle around the park with my helmet on to stay safe.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A bicycle is a fun vehicle with two wheels that you pedal with your feet.
nouns.fun_fact: The first bicycle had no pedals - you pushed it with your feet on the ground!

nouns.meaning: A round object used in many games and sports like soccer or basketball
nouns.example: I kick the red ball into the soccer goal and score a point for my team! Hooray!
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A ball is a round toy that you can throw, kick, bounce, or catch.
nouns.fun_fact: The largest ball of paint has over 20,000 layers of paint!

nouns.meaning: A set of pages with words and pictures that are stuck together inside a cover
nouns.example: My favorite book has a friendly dragon, a brave princess, and a funny knight.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A book has stories, pictures, and information inside that you can read.
nouns.fun_fact: The longest book ever written would take over 100 days to read!

nouns.meaning: An object for children to play with and have fun
nouns.example: My new toy is a robot that lights up, makes sounds, and even walks across the floor.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A toy is something fun that you play with, like dolls, cars, blocks, or stuffed animals.
nouns.fun_fact: The oldest toy ever found is over 4,000 years old!

nouns.meaning: A small toy shaped like a person or baby
nouns.example: I brush my doll's long hair and dress her in pretty purple clothes.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A doll is a toy that looks like a person, and you can dress her up and play pretend.
nouns.fun_fact: The most expensive doll ever sold cost over $6 million!

nouns.meaning: A soft stuffed toy shaped like a bear
nouns.example: I hug my teddy bear every night when I go to sleep because he is so soft.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A teddy bear is a cuddly stuffed animal shaped like a bear.
nouns.fun_fact: Teddy bears were named after President Theodore 'Teddy' Roosevelt!

nouns.meaning: A person you like and enjoy spending time with
nouns.example: My best friend sits next to me at lunch every day and we share our snacks.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A friend is someone you like to play with, talk to, and share things with.
nouns.fun_fact: Having friends makes you happier and healthier!

nouns.meaning: A group of people related to each other, living together or not
nouns.example: My family has my mom, my dad, my little sister, and me, plus our dog Max.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: Your family is the people who love you, take care of you, and live with you.
nouns.fun_fact: Families come in all sizes - some are big, some are small!

nouns.meaning: Your female parent who takes care of you
nouns.example: My mom gives me a big hug and a kiss on the forehead every morning before school.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: Mom is the lady who is your parent and loves you very much.
nouns.fun_fact: The word 'mom' is similar in almost every language!

nouns.meaning: Your male parent who takes care of you
nouns.example: My dad makes fluffy pancakes with maple syrup on Sunday mornings for breakfast.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: Dad is the man who is your parent and loves you very much.
nouns.fun_fact: Some animal dads, like seahorses, even have the babies!

nouns.meaning: Your mother's mother or father's mother
nouns.example: My grandma bakes the best chocolate chip cookies and tells me stories about when she was little.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: Grandma is your parent's mom. She loves to spoil you!
nouns.fun_fact: Grandmas give the best hugs in the whole world!

nouns.meaning: Your mother's father or father's father
nouns.example: My grandpa teaches me how to fish and tells funny jokes that make me laugh.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: Grandpa is your parent's dad. He loves to play with you.
nouns.fun_fact: Some grandpas can do amazing magic tricks!

nouns.meaning: A person whose job is to help children learn at school
nouns.example: My teacher reads us exciting stories, teaches us math, and helps us when we are stuck.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A teacher helps you learn new things like reading, writing, and math.
nouns.fun_fact: Teachers help over 1 billion children learn every day!

nouns.meaning: A place where children go to learn from teachers
nouns.example: I go to school from Monday to Friday to learn reading, math, science, and art.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: School is a building where you go to learn new things and play with friends.
nouns.fun_fact: The largest school in the world has over 50,000 students!

nouns.meaning: The special day every year when you celebrate the day you were born
nouns.example: On my birthday, I eat delicious cake, open presents, and play games with my friends.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: Your birthday is the special day you celebrate getting one year older.
nouns.fun_fact: The oldest person ever lived to be 122 years old!

nouns.meaning: A gift given to someone for a birthday, holiday, or just because
nouns.example: I got a new puzzle and a coloring book as presents for my seventh birthday.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A present is something nice that someone gives you to make you happy.
nouns.fun_fact: The biggest present ever weighed over 100 tons!

nouns.meaning: A sweet baked food often decorated with frosting and eaten at parties
nouns.example: The chocolate cake has colorful sprinkles, a cherry on top, and a single candle.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: Cake is a sweet, yummy dessert with frosting, often eaten at birthday parties.
nouns.fun_fact: The tallest cake ever was over 100 feet tall - as tall as a 10-story building!

nouns.meaning: A clear, tasteless liquid that people, animals, and plants need to live
nouns.example: I drink cold water when I am thirsty because it is healthy and makes me feel good.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: Water is the clear drink that comes from the faucet or a bottle. You need it to live.
nouns.fun_fact: Your body is about 60% water!

nouns.meaning: The big bright star in our sky that gives us light and warmth
nouns.example: The warm sun feels nice on my face when I play outside on a summer day.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: The sun is the big, round, yellow thing in the sky that makes daytime bright and warm.
nouns.fun_fact: The sun is so big that over 1 million Earths could fit inside it!
